title: Convergence to Stationary Distribution in a Regular Markov Chain
description: A visual flowchart illustrating how a regular Markov chain converges from an initial state distribution to a stationary distribution over time, regardless of the starting state, and contrasts this with absorbing Markov chains where states can be trapped.
elements: Absorbing State (A state that, once entered, cannot be left)
Transient State (A state that is not absorbing)
relationships: π₀ is multiplied by P repeatedly to produce πₙ
As n → ∞, πₙ converges to π for a regular Markov chain
π is the eigenvector of P with eigenvalue 1
In a chain with absorbing states, probability gets 'trapped' and flows into the absorbing states
A regular Markov chain has no absorbing states and is irreducible and aperiodic
